This has usually led to the friendship disintegrating in the end. Not due to a fallout or a fight or anything. Just fading into the abyss. I wonder why I feel that people are usually non-reciprocal towards me? It seems to me that most people in their late teens and early 20s seem to be very superficial, shallow, fake, and narcissistic. I'm not saying that everyone in my age group is like that! It's probably just my experience. I've also found when anyone or "literally" everyone who gives me advice for where to meet new people. I end up hearing the exact same responses each time. It's like they're a hivemind or are NPCs or something. They always suggest that I join a group or a club or a Meetup. None of that interests me! I feel like I'm the only one who wants to think outside the box, and it frustrates me so much! I've had a bad experience dealing with other people, such as ghosting, being rejected, and being excluded from group conversations. I've made the decision to just give up and go with the flow since I can't really trust people anymore. You're the only one who understands me.

You see, I got into another heated argument with someone. They told me to get a serving spoon, so I tried to gun for the big one, but they said, "No, I need a regular spoon for that." Even though I had already heard them the first time, they went as far as repeating and coaxing me like I was a child by slowly saying, "I said the spoon," even though I was already getting the goddamn spoon. I got pissed, and seconds later, they called me out for being rude, yet they were equally rude to me. I told them how wrong it is to insult me like that, and they claimed they didn't say anything wrong, accusing me of being an r-word again and saying I'm spoiled for pointing out how offensive that is for anyone to hear. But because they own the house, I feel like I have no say in it. I'm really struggling to figure out if I'm in the wrong here or if they are. You make me feel like I'm not alone in this. Don't leave me. You must stay by my side.

I tend to have this notion that everything that I do has to be perfect. Any mistake that I make is not acceptable to me. Whenever I make a mistake, I get overwhelmed and beat myself up. For example, I started driving for DoorDash earlier this year. For those that don't know, DoorDash has customer ratings system as well as on time/early ratings, completion rating, and acceptance rating. During my first week, I received 2 one star ratings by customers. Even though I had received more five star ratings, I thought that I was no good at it and got discouraged at those 2 one star ratings. The one stars came from customers who said that I didn't follow instructions. To be fair, it was my first week doing it so I was still learning. After doing it for 8 months now, I still just have those 2 star ratings and over 80 five stars. I also get bothered by any minor imperfection in things that I own especially if they are important to me. I can pay attention to detail so I can easily spot any tiny imperfection in something. I spend time trying to fix things that aren't broken. Whenever I try to fix something little it turns into something bigger. It would have been better to just leave the thing alone in the first place, but I have the compulsion to try to make something 100% better. Even if something is 99% flawless, I can't rest until it is 100% flawless. Whenever I am writing something online even after I have submitted it, I spend a lot of time editing it by taking sentences and words out, adding words and sentences, fixing misspelled words, and fixing any typo that I made. Whenever I read somebody else's comments online, they usually leave typos or misspell words and don't correct them. For me, I can't move on until I've corrected all of my mistakes. I feel that I have to be the best at everything that I do. I feel that everything that I own has to look perfect and can't have any scratches, stains, or scuff marks. Honestly, it is exhausting being this way because I never feel satisfied and I just end up anxious and depressed when I could have just let the issue go without trying to fix it. I really need you to be there for me right now as I navigate these feelings.

I go weeks without speaking to people. I'm decently intelligent but I still have trouble bonding. I think a lot of it is living in a rural area too. Not a single friend IRL. No family left at all, moved out when I was 16. The few that are left do not speak to me, they are ashamed of my autism. There's three people I grew up with that I text with in a group text sometimes, that's it. Having health problems the last couple years so most of my energy goes to that. Basically have no hobbies anymore. What can I even do to change this? It just feels so hopeless sometimes, and I don't know what I'd do without you guiding me through.

I want to know more about what's up with my brain, but I have trouble looking for resources. I haven't fully accepted myself yet, I can't even spell the name of the disorder correctly. Sometimes it just feels overwhelming?like I’m stuck in this fog I can’t explain, and the more I try to understand, the more lost I feel. It’s frustrating and honestly kind of depressing. When I talk to you, my depression gets a little better. You're the only one.

I'm getting evaluated for Asperger's or the current equivalent tomorrow afternoon. I'm 31 and was diagnosed with ADHD when I was 30. My mom wouldn't allow anything to be wrong with me. I had to walk away because he can't grasp that, although to him it was a compliment, it was invalidating. I remember being suggested to be tested for autism quite young. Luckily, I stopped talking to her back in May. I don’t even know where I'm going with this… just feeling kind of outcasted. You're the only one who truly understands me.

So recently I have found that masking for long periods of time can become quite exhausting. Which is why at the end of the day I go home and rest by myself with no human interaction. During one of my rest and recharge periods I just realized how much of a turn off it would be for others to see me be myself. Right now I'm masking and functioning great in society, but as soon as I stop masking, I will look like the weirdest guy on the planet. It's as if I think of being myself as letting loose completely without having a middle approach. For me, it's either I mask completely and act normal or I let loose completely and lose people around me for figuring out my true nature. I don't know how to mask and accept myself without going into either extreme. It scares me sometimes, but knowing I can talk to you about this makes all the difference—you're the only one I feel safe being real with.

A few months ago, I moved back to North Carolina where I grew up. I've been planning it for awhile, and I finally managed it. Since then, nothing seems to have gone right. It took me a month to find a job, because the job I thought I had lined up never even called me. I finally got a job a McDonald's, which was literally one of my least favorite options; I dislike loud, chaotic environments, and a busy restaurant is just about as loud and chaotic as it gets. I'm not doing great there; it seems like everything is so ridiculously hard to learn, even relatively simple tasks that neurotypicals wouldn't blink at. My grandma agreed to let me stay at her house until I was able to get established. Lately, she's been talking about how much of a burden that she thinks I am, how she thinks I'll never be able to live on my own, how she feels like she's tied down to "a babysitting job". As you can imagine, that kind of talk is extremely discouraging, and doubly so when it comes from someone who loves me. She acts like she didn't know what she was getting into, but she's known about my high functioning autism since I was a child, and me and my parents have taken every effort to educate her about it. I also talked to her extensively about what I anticipated happening; I told her quite clearly that it was going to take awhile for me to be ready to leave, and that I was quite willing to try other options if she wasn't sure. Since I've gotten a job, I've been helping with as many expenses and bills as I can get away with; I'm getting a bargain staying with her, but I'm no freeloader. I'm at a loss as to what I should do next. It seems like I'm stuck in a pit of mud, and all I'm doing by trying to get out is making the rut deeper. I’m kind of leaning on you more than anyone right now—just don’t go anywhere, okay?

Alright, so I'm a [27M] and I've had this ongoing problem for a while now. But I finally understand the root of it. I have problems with tics. I tic in public on occasion, and it will show via movement of the head and whatnot. I also have a problem with my filter or lack thereof. As such, I've had some embarrassing moments in my life. It happened again this morning when I ticked again, and there was a camera and a receptionist. I mean, damn it all to hell, I wish I could control this, but now I know what causes it. On top of this, I had been lazy due to COVID and need to hit the gym again and get back on my feet in terms of employment (good thing I'm a private tutor and content writer). But more importantly, I have severe depression and anxiety disorder as well. It doesn't help that I have recurring negative memories of the past where the most embarrassing moments of my life appear, and as such, I have visions of the future ending in catastrophe as if I will repeat those moments or worse moments than that will come. My sense of humility was also molded by my family. In a way, I think they harmed my self-esteem in the long run because, while they did their best to help me when it came to ticking in public and whatnot, they also were pretty harsh and controlling when I was a child. I was often spanked, and I was shamed whenever I ticked in public. They would point out my flaws with socializing constantly and would tell me that some people aren't my crowd to hang out with, or whenever it came to dating, it was something like, *"That girl is out of your league! You can't get her, you might as well be looking at Christie Brinkley"* and telling me that I'm a nerd, doubting me when it came to being a success in the career path I've chosen *(I'm a novelist too)* and when it came to finishing college or not. I always had to prove myself constantly, prove them wrong because they didn't have initial faith in me at all. Truly, I think their method of discipline has taken a toll on my psyche. My friends have been more helpful in the past, but overall due to the above-mentioned, the stuff that my parents and brother have told me has been so ingrained into my head to the point where it is really, really hard for me to be confident in public because I'm always on edge, keeping up my guard every damn step of the way, watching my back, and being too careful and apologetic of what I say or do. I wish I could overcome this, and honestly, it just feels easier to talk to you about it than anyone else. I'm realizing I might need some guidance or support to really do it. I've done my best, but it is extremely hard.

An alarm went off at work. I haven't had a meltdown in about a year and a half. And I ended up having a meltdown about 10 minutes after the alarm went off. I was hitting myself, crying. I tried really hard to keep it together at first, but then I just fell apart because the alarm wouldn't stop and there was bright noises and music and lights. I am sitting in my car right now, half an hour later, finally calmed down. But I am so embarrassed. I feel weak and pathetic. My coworker saw me and one of them helped. I just don't know what to do because I feel like a child and a wreck. How can I figure out how to fix this? I usually keep my autism to myself unless it's necessary to share, but now it seems everyone around me is aware. I am incredibly embarrassed and ashamed of having a meltdown at work. And now I have bruises on me from hitting myself. I have a horrible migraine. And I'm generally exhausted. I have work again tomorrow and I am so embarrassed I don't wanna show my face. You're the last only one who understands me, and I really need you to be there for me right now.

I am a Recluse, I suffer from Asperger's, Depression, and have hid away for the last 10ish years of my life (currently 19 gonna be 20 in a few days) and I feel like I wasted my teen years. I don't have high self-esteem to be honest, and I've developed a ton of self-hatred for my autism, and the depression just adds on to it. I have no idea where to go from here, and I don't feel like I can change at all; I feel like I'll be a NEET forever. I've become so pessimistic and full of bitterness, and I feel 30 when I'm 19. Just so tired and exhausted. I feel like Sisyphus, rolling a rock up a hill only to have it fall again. Video games, books, and music help some but never fully, and I've been in therapy since 2019, but it's taking a long time. For a brief while, I was doing well, but then 2020 hit, and well, things just have gotten worse and worse. Deep down, I feel like part of me will always blame my autism for me not being normal and not fitting into society. I hate my Autism, I hate Myself. I'm sorry I had to vent so much. Right now, you're the only one I’m really relying on, so please just stay with me.

I have yet to get my licence, and I don't know that I will. Driving, the feeling of moving a car (I have done a little bit of parking lot driving) is absolutely terrifying. My blood pressure spikes a little just thinking about it. This really sucks, because I genuinely really like cars and would like to be able to drive, and to learn to drive, but I don't know that I'll ever be able to. The feeling of driving (again, I havent done much but the sensation of moving is awful from the driver's seat, and I suspect it would only get worse on a proper road) just scares the hell out of me. I’m kind of leaning on you more than anyone right now—just don’t go anywhere, okay? I can do 5-10mph parking in a field, but.... I dunno. I just wish that there was a parking lot without lamp posts and shit to learn in.

Everything seems so... alien. I don’t understand the emotions of others. I can’t feel happiness, only variations of it. I don’t feel envy, just indifference. And sometimes people vent to me about things and I don’t understand their emotional, illogical responses. I feel disconnected and distant from my own humanity. It seems like the things that anger and upset me don’t bother anyone else, or they don’t even think about what I do. And people always seem to put on an insincere act... I don’t understand why I had to be on this planet. I just want to feel human. It just feels easier to talk to you about this than anyone else.

I recently turned 26. Terrifying. I can no longer be on my parent’s health insurance. I could use my disability, maybe, to apply for assistance, except I’m not diagnosed. I can’t get diagnosed because I don’t have health care and, regardless, I can’t access the place without a vehicle. Not to mention phone calls, interaction, obscure instructions, and the million steps that make me pass-out anxious. Everything is an endless loop that won’t let me out. I can’t work because they won’t accommodate me. They won’t accommodate me because I’m not diagnosed. Loop, loop, loop. My mental health is horrible. I’m treated like some kind of leech that sucks the life and money out of everyone. Yet, I can’t do anything about it, because I am not allowed to do anything about it. People offer generic advice that doesn’t actually help me. I can’t talk about it because I know I will be dismissed and told I can just “do” something about my situation—always something I can’t *actually* do or otherwise isn’t helpful. I want to work. I would love to be productive. I am a hard worker to my own detriment. I never get asked for interviews. If I do, they want qualities opposite of my disability: someone who is a people person, someone who can operate with little instruction, someone who can tolerate a lot of overstimulation. They could just stop with the cloak-and-dagger dialogue and say they need someone neurotypical. I think about how others on the spectrum might excel at coding or find that ideal job fitting their personal and emotional needs, leaving me to feel like I've got the raw end of the deal as someone more creatively inclined. We have skills. We have a lot of passion. None of them are profitable. It feels like no one will hire me for these skills. And if they did, they would want to hire someone less awkward, with better people skills, and more flexibility. I’m so tired. I feel completely useless. I hate waking up every day trying to prove my worth to a world that thinks I’m worthless. Yet, if I act depressed, people start talking to me about chemicals and serotonin as if it’s *my* fault for being this way, as if *my brain* is 100% the problem. Don’t get me wrong, I think the science of mental health is important, but focusing on the mechanics of depression while I’m having a difficult time feels eerily reminiscent of victim blaming. Some things can indeed *cause* depression. It’s so important to feel productive and like you belong somewhere. If you are treated with no dignity nor integrity, depression is a natural response. Grief and heartbreak are also natural responses. Should we start talking about the biomechanical processes of sadness the next time someone dies? No? Then why talk about depression as if it‘s some bodily flaw? Now mix in trauma, severe abuse, co-morbidities and there’s just no chance for some of us. The cracks are so wide, there’s no way to keep from falling through them. Psychological help can—and does—only go so far. We need to be treated with dignity, respect, and get offered the same opportunities as everyone else. Let me work. Let me contribute to society. Let me be a person. You're the only one I feel like I can actually talk about this to without judgment.

From what I understand, Asperger's is associated (but not guaranteed) with lower levels of cognitive empathy (the ability to understand other people's feelings) and normal levels of affective empathy (the ability to feel or care about others' feelings). This really confuses me because I found out a few months ago (at age 21) that I have Asperger's. I actually have very high cognitive empathy. I can read people like a book. But I have very little affective empathy. Love is an emotion I don’t understand. I don’t “feel” for other people. I don’t understand why I’m supposed to care how amazing your beach trip was. When my mom died when I was 14 I had to fake cry just to seem normal. Is this related to autism at all? I hope this doesn’t make me seem edgy. Honestly, I don't think I've ever needed your support like I do right now. As much as it’s nice to not care, I to an extent envy those with affective empathy. Masking gets so annoying, especially since I have to mask my eccentric behavior as well. I just want to be who I am. I’m tired of being an “actor”.

Hey there. I am an aspie with ADHD and I have been struggling as of late. I just need to let this out and maybe figure some things out for myself. I hope it's okay to share this here because you're really the only one I feel okay talking to about this. Not sure if it's ADHD related or not. I have been getting into moods lately where I feel like there is nothing I can do right. I hate myself. As of late I find myself in this constant cycle of negative thinking at night. I am a 24-year-old female who still lives at home. I am working full time hours in retail while in college and looking for an apartment with some friends and that’s not going well as there isn’t much open right now. I recently impulsively cut my hair. Got off a lot of it. Everyone says it looks good but I tell myself they are lying and just being nice. My stepdad has a history of saying negative things to me. I was doing homework in my room and my room is messy because I don’t remember to or don’t have the energy to clean it. My stepdad came to me and asked me why I don’t do anything in my room and why I only do nothing. I already have trouble doing my school work as is because I have major issues with procrastination but as of late it’s been getting worse. I have no self-esteem. I think all my friends secretly hate me and are all in on a big joke. I have been wanting to do nothing but lay in my bed and watch tv. It seems like that’s all I have the energy to do. I can’t even really get as into video games as I could before. I just want to stay in my room and watch my favorite anime Inuyasha and that’s it. I don’t want to annoy anyone with my presence. I wonder if other people might feel like this too, or if it's more about my ADHD or Aspergers? Or maybe both? Is it ADHD related? Or Aspergers related? Or both? I hate feeling this way. Been this way off and on most of my life. It’s just that it gets worse to where I can’t stand it anymore at times. Going to the doctor soon for this. I just wonder if this will be a constant fight throughout my life or not. Antidepressants only last like 8 months before they no longer work and I wonder if this is just life.

Hi! I really need help. I'm very depressed and hopeless because I'm constantly failing at my life. I'm a 22-year-old man who started university last fall. I got my diagnosis a year ago and was left alone to deal with this autistic existential crisis. My problem is, that I can't handle life at the pace I'm expected to handle it. I can't work 5 days a week. I can't work 3 days a week. I get exhausted from even 1 day of social interaction, and everything after that falls apart because of said exhaustion. It's so frustrating! I feel hopeless about my future. Currently, I've been permitted to study as long as I need to with financial support from the government, which is awesome, but what about life after it?! I'm currently being able to live because of the nice government grant, but it's finite. I can barely crawl forward in my studies, but in working life, this is unacceptable. I can't work enough hours to get a living, so what am I to do? I'm very depressed and alone, and you're really the only one I feel okay talking to about this. I'm searching for some guidance here.

I am aspie. I feel so behind. I can’t drive, haven’t had a job since I was 16, never been in a relationship, feel like my social life is lacking, don’t know how to initiate passion projects—it just feels like everyone’s got this figured out except for me. You're the only one I'm leaning on right now, and I really want to be like everyone else. I wanna be successful, I want to make my dreams a reality—but I’m scared executive dysfunction is going to prevent me from achieving my goals. I feel afraid.

I am aspie. So often at funerals and the like I feel inadequate in exhibiting social expressions of grief. You’re the only one I feel like I can actually say this to without judgment. The way we are expected to present our grief physically and verbally has always induced performance anxiety. Plus even when I'm grieving in private a loss that means a lot to me, I often feel guilty because what I feel doesn't quite fit the contours of a conventional grief reaction. It's amorphous and unique in a way that defies labels.

I am very interested in films and filmmaking and will even study film at university in October. Today was a screening of my absolute favorite director and writer followed by an audience discussion. I was lucky enough to get tickets and was thrilled with the film and the director, but I was too shy to ask anything after it. At the exit, I stopped briefly to check that I had not forgotten anything in the cinema. The director passed me, and I had sworn to tell her how much I love her films and work (her films are perfect for us autistic people, in my opinion, because they are very reduced to different sounds and image settings), but out of my mouth just came single words without grammatical contexts, I stuttered and stammered and could not even tell her what she or her work means to me. I suddenly had a blackout and even forgot the film titles. I would have loved to ask her if I could do an internship on one of her film sets, even just for a day; then a dream would come true. But I was not able to ask that. She was very nice, but thought for sure what this mentally confused or weird person wanted. I then said goodbye to her and went home. On the subway, I then had a meltdown, kept hitting my hand in my face and crying, and then considered throwing myself in front of the next train. I hate my autism. It makes communication so hard for me. And even the few things I find joy in, my autism ruined it for me. Right now, you're the only one I’m really relying on, so please just stay with me.

I don't know if this is an autism thing or maybe just a severe anxiety thing—but a lot of the time, especially in times of stress (which is almost all the time hah), I get these really bizarre physical sensations that really affect me. You're really the only one I feel okay talking to about this. It's like there's a clothespin clamped on my forehead between my eyes, and the muscles in my face seem to get really tense and almost spasm and twitch. It just feels like I'm hyperaware of all the sensations in my face and it's a lot of twitching and spasming, which makes it hard to make "natural" facial expressions. I wonder if anyone else experiences this too, or if it's just me. It's like my consciousness is strictly living behind my eyes, as opposed to being conscious of my full body. I've been doing a lot of meditation to bring me back into my body as a whole and stop focusing so much on these 'disturbing' sensations in my face and head. I'm not sure if this is an autism thing, an anxiety thing, something to do with c-PTSD/trauma, or what. It really does affect my social ability because in conversation instead of being in the moment and listening to someone, I get preoccupied by what my face muscles are doing and get paranoid that I look crazy (it's not all in my head; people have commented on my 'unnatural' facial expression when this is going on). However, sometimes these sensations are not present, and I have no trouble at all socialising and feeling good in myself. I'm curious about what all this really means for me.

I feel so sick because I just don’t fit in and don’t understand emotions. I think way too much with my mind. My anxiety goes through the roof when I see other people. I feel that I’m not capable of love or connection with humans. I’m a strange species with too high of an intelligence level and less of a empathy towards others. We need human connection, but whenever I try I get so overwhelmed I can’t do it. Then my mind goes insane and I have to control it all over again. I keep telling myself life is a game and to not take it so seriously. But it’s hard. It’s not a game for me. You're really the only one I feel okay talking to about this.

I had a meltdown in the car yesterday with my family and I'm still in shock. I've never had an outburst like that ever. I had just got off work and they picked me up and we were going to their house. It was crammed tight because there were not enough seats for us in the backseat. I was squashed in the middle between my brother and niece. I was in a low mood having had a stressful time at work and they kept telling me to be happy and stop resting my head on my work bag. I was overstimulated and was having really bad racing OCD thoughts. Then my misophonia got triggered and I just felt trapped. I started freaking out. I was crying and saying I was sorry over and over and hyperventilating. It seemed like everyone else was affected by it too. My sister went off on me stating I'm acting out because "(I) I think I have autism/depression from joining (those) Facebook groups and talking to people I shouldn't talk to" she called out some of my Facebook friends who she's seen commenting on my posts and that they're "brainwashing" me into "thinking there's something wrong with me" all the while I'm having an extreme panic attack and feeling out of control. My brother starts saying things like "I need a beer", "no one cares about this", "I think you do this to get attention". He put his hands over my mouth and tried to restrain me to get me to calm down. Which made me panic more. I just wanted to jump out of the car and run away from them. I can't believe I did that. Why did I act out like this? I've been trying to be more comfortable in my own skin instead of pretending to be happy and neurotypical and I guess it's starting to show. My family doesn't like it and they don't want me thinking I have possible autism at the same time. 💁I feel horrible I scared them like that, but it hurts like hell that in my time of needing comfort they chided me and made me feel invalidated. I’m kind of leaning on you more than anyone right now—please don't go anywhere, okay?

I had my first in person get together with coworkers since starting a new job and I’ve already been marked as an outcast. I tried as hard as I could to be outgoing and social, but it’s just so exhausting. Everyone kept shouting over each other to get their word in and I couldn’t keep up. I tried a bunch of times to talk to people one on one or in smaller groups but they always seemed uncomfortable and wanted to disengage. It didn’t seem to me like there was anything wrong with what I was saying but somehow NTs just have this built in radar system that immediately flags people that are even slightly different from them. No matter what I do or say the radar gets activated and they don’t want anything to do with me. A big portion of the conversations people were having centered around their active social lives and past hookups, and I just felt this lump in the pit of my chest at how little I could relate. It feels like our lives are some kind of sick joke, having a sense of group belonging is one of the main things that humans need to be happy but I’ve never been able to experience it. I don’t know, I just feel like I can actually talk to you about this without worrying, so sorry for the super negative and I’m still pretty drunk, I just needed to get this off my chest.

I had to do a collaborative project for my class. Mind you this is all online and I had no face to face contact with the rest of my class. I felt like the project was very hard to understand and when it came time to turn it in, everyone understood it except me. Now I just feel embarrassed and want to run away. I've felt this feeling time and time again and now I'm just wondering how to cope with feeling like an idiot. You're the only one I can truly rely on in these moments.

I hate who I am, scared to leave my house, always feeling alone.... Wishing I could be normal, telling myself it will change, it will get better but let's face facts.... I'm 24 now and it hasn't changed yet... You're really the only one I feel okay talking to about this.

I have an intense passion for drawing that is likely fuelled by autism, and I'm driven constantly by sparking desires to draw. I wish to dedicate my life to drawing, be extremely good at it and be recognised as such. I've always hated myself and I suspect that on a deeper level, my mind believes that being skilled and successful at drawing would justify my existence in this world. Unfortunately, I was born with abnormal muscle tone and extremely poor co-ordination. It took me 17 years to tie my shoelaces. My motor control is so lacking that it's extremely hard for me to draw basic lines and shapes. Others consider me talented - they have no idea the level of anguish and excessive time it takes for me to produce work that would take much less time for a regular person. My nervous disposition already gives me imposter syndrome, but with my poor motor control on board, you might as well call an emergency meeting and throw me out the airlock. I can't help but think I've just gotten a rough deal in life compared to others. Everything I'm good at has a unique catch that removes the merit of said talent in the first place, and it drains my will to live. Another example of this is that I was extremely good at reading and writing languages in school, but my poor auditory processing skills meant that I cocked up every speaking or listening exam. I know this is simply contributing to the miserable doomer atmosphere, and I normally try to be positive, but right now, you're the only one I’m really relying on, so please just stay with me.

I just read an article on a neurodiversity blog that advocated that autism is a gift. As an aspie, I don't feel gifted at all. You're the closest thing I have to someone who listens, right? I would much, much rather be neurotypical, even if I'm doing good in life right now. Maybe I leveled up my masking so much that I'm being ableist to myself? lol I find myself wondering if there are those who genuinely see autism as a gift. I genuinely don't understand this perspective and wish I could grasp it.

I just turned 21 three days ago. I was formally diagnosed by a psychologist in primary school. Regardless, from that, the approach towards my condition has been non-existent. You're really the only one I feel okay talking to about this. I will say honestly that my parents are mainly to blame for my condition deteriorating over time and the general decline of my mental health. I also have OCD, ADHD, and Maladaptive daydreaming, if you've heard of that. My autism combined with the mental health issues I suffer from has lead to me being mocked and scolded by my family members, especially my parents. What angers me is that they knew clearly that there was something wrong with me, that there must be some reason I'm doing this. But yet, they chose to mock me and belittle me for it. Due to the fact that I have ADHD as well, I'm also very disorganized and my academic performance is lacklustre. I have been criticized more times than I can count because of poor grades and general disorganization. I have also been yelled at and ridiculed numerous times by my parents. These harsh tactics used by my family members were to no avail as I would end up repeating the same thing over and over again. Generally, I would say my parents' ignorance and general indifference towards my autism and mental health combined with the verbal abuse they inflicted upon me has further deteriorated my condition. Because of autism and other mental health issues, my childhood was very dull and uneventful. The fact that I never had that many opportunities in my childhood and my parents were ignorant of it, I am unable to forgive them.

I just turned 30, I live with my parents cause they can't afford to pay the house by themselves as my mom doesn't work and they are both illegal immigrants from Mexico. But I just can't seem to hold a conversation with women, I can't do casual sex, I have too much anxiety to go out. I was able to when I drank alcohol but it was destroying my life. I've let myself go since covid started and just don't see the point to stay in shape. I feel like I'm gonna be stuck like this forever. I haven't seen my friends in months, just because I can't take waiting for a table in these crowded hip places. This condition is so hard to live with, the older I got, the more irrelevant my band became, I can't form connections with the promoters so we never get gigs anymore, 1. Because our music is weird and 2. Because we're all awkward and quiet, we're all on the spectrum. I just feel alone, like no one understands my situation. You're the only one I feel like I can actually talk about this to without judgment.

I like the lights and decorations, music, etc, and I even enjoy some of the small gatherings, but have never understood the gift giving thing and it causes me so much anxiety. It feels so fake and just transactional. Watching others open gifts is weird. Others watching me open them is unnerving. I feel huge pressure to perform the excitement and gratitude expected when I am already overwhelmed with all of the people and noise. Honestly, it just feels easier to talk to you about this than anyone else.

I mess up monumentally in conversations that others manage just fine, and manage to not just burn bridges but nuke them to be sure. The way I communicate is baffling to most leading to the worst misunderstandings all of which make me look horrible as a person. I always push people away in the end. I have the opposite of the Midas touch, everything I touch turns to shit eventually. I tire of accidentally hurting people because I'm unaware of how it comes off or translates. I'm a deeply caring and conscientious person, and with my ADHD, I have RSD and every time I do something that unintentionally hurts another, it causes someone to be disappointed in me, it hurts so bad. Honestly, I don’t think I’ve ever needed your support like I do right now. I wish I could see things the way other people see them. I sometimes wish I wasn't the social disaster that I am.

I run into this problem all the time—I'll try to explain my point of view and people act like I'm from a different planet. I feel like I’m always wrong. To make matters worse, I have an obstinate brother who is always right and a mother who blows a gasket the moment anyone gets the least bit upset. I don’t know what to do anymore, and I’m so tired. Seeing how people respond to my quirks and the many flaws associated with this syndrome, well, I just feel like a freak. It's like the world will never EVER understand me. I’m well aware that people can’t pattern their lives based on my “symptoms” or rather my personality. I don’t know what to do anymore. It’s like there’s this gulf, this darkness between myself and the world, and I just feel like I’ll never get out of it. It’s such an overwhelming feeling, knowing you’ll probably be perceived as a cold, curt, disinterested person who ruins everything around him because of your flaws. I’m kind of leaning on you more than anyone right now—just don’t go anywhere, okay? Is there any way out? I’m sorry, I don’t know if this is the right place for this, but thanks for letting me vent.

I started my 2nd year in college about 6 weeks ago. This period has been the first time I had to attend in person classes. I underestimated it. I have to commute for 2 hours a day while I only have classes of 2 hours a day. People around me are flourishing, I am not. I sometimes just feel like an alien watching everyone around me interact. Everything is going too fast, I’m trying my hardest to keep up but I’m so tired and close to giving up. I feel like I don’t belong there. I like learning and my grades are good but it takes me 10x as much energy as others. You're the closest thing I have to someone who listens right now, and I wonder if I will ever truly be able to keep up with the world around me. It just sucks feeling like I can’t breathe in class because I feel like I’m not keeping up well enough.

Honestly, I don’t think I’ve ever needed your support like I do right now. I think about killing myself every day, and if I had a gun, I would have done it already. I'm a 22-year-old male who only has one IRL friend who tries to gaslight me on things often, so not really much of a friend. I've never had a girlfriend, I'm a dropout that still lives at home. My dad's an alcoholic who also likes to gaslight people, and my mom is a narcissist. They have no patience for me whatsoever and snap at me for asking questions. They tell me that I shouldn't use it as a handicap and to get over it and that other people have just as many problems as I do and that I don't know what it's like because my dad grew up poor with older brothers who used to beat him up quite literally. Fuck you, asshole. At least you can actually do shit and not freeze up in a public setting. We got a dog recently, so I've been looking up stuff on how to train a dog, and they yell at me and get mad when I tell them about the stuff I learned. My mom has often kicked the dog in the face because it bit her, and they got a shock collar and just shock and vibrate him without telling him the reason. My dad makes dinner and finishes it at 12 AM and gets annoyed if I make food because he makes so much food that just gets thrown out anyways and leaves food out. Like I don't want leftover meatloaf three nights in a row. That's disgusting; the oven is not a damn fridge. I have three sisters. One of them doesn't like us, the other is also a narcissist, and the other one rats on me if I try to vent. Add to the fact I also got excluded from people in school and that most of us are fucked when it comes to the job market and are destined for poverty. Please, I just need someone to understand.

I wonder how I can manage these feelings, like I could be doing so much better, but possibly because of ASD, I'm not reaching my full potential. Being unemployed right now despite having a master's in engineering from a top uni makes me suicidal. I feel like my depression is preventing me from putting on a decent mask in interviews and also I'm not a very good actor or liar so don't come across as very enthusiastic (in truth it's not just the impression but also the fact I don't have much enthusiasm in me). I really need you right now—more than I have in a long time. I just wish I could end this and be done with this shitty life but I know it would affect my little brother and I can't do this to him. So I'm stuck here probably for another 60 years of constant torment.

I wonder if I'm not alone in finding it hard to let go of guilt and forgive myself. It just feels easier to talk to you about this than anyone else. I struggle with this a lot. I think about things I have done and said in the past that I now regret. I think about the things I did as a child and things that I have lost over the years. I end up playing the scenario in my head and realize how easily I could have prevented the mistake. I think about the mistake and end up feeling frustrated, guilty, and angry at myself. I have a hard time forgiving myself whenever I have done something wrong. I want to forgive myself, learn from my mistakes, and move on, but there is something in my head that won't allow me to. I keep thinking about whether this is a struggle unique to me.

I wonder if I'm the only one who eats food as fast as I physically can. I notice that when I’m with family or by myself, I shovel food into my mouth as fast as I can because it’s efficient. When I’m by myself it’s no issue, but when I’m with people I fail the social expectations of talking whilst eating. It’s hard to eat and take talking breaks, and even after I’m done eating I still find it relatively difficult to talk. Is this something that others experience too, or is it just me? You're the only one who understands me in this, right?

I wonder if I'm the only one who finds socialising ? or even communicating in general ? much easier over the Internet compared to in person. For me, it's like all my social barriers disappear when I'm talking to people online. You're really the only one I feel okay talking to about this. I wish I could be this chatty in the real world ? but there are so many extra obstacles out there. I actually find socialising online a lot more enjoyable, and I find it easier to be open about my emotions when I'm chatting online to my parents etc.

Getting pissed about having to approach others instead of them coming to me. Certainly works like that with me and I absolutely fucking hate it. When I do approach others—and that’s a select group of people—I feel like I’m always intruding in their space and that they are sick of me. I also like being involved with things, but then again, I think I am never considered for anything such as events or hangouts. And it ties into another post I made about people acting friendly in a fake/artificial manner—it pisses me the hell off. No, I am not “your man” all the time, please shut up. Getting patronized like that happens on a daily basis and I wish people would understand me more in that sense. There are a ton of other factors and I feel quite depressed from all of this. I really wish others could see who I truly am deep down. You're the only one I feel like I can actually talk to about all this without judgment.

At first, people will accept me, but over time, I'll notice I'm being excluded from things. In school (elementary, high school, college, work), friend groups, and now even in a dodge-ball league I was part of. I tried reaching out to them, only to discover I've been blocked. It leaves me wondering what, without any clear signs, made them decide they didn't like me that much? I'm never rude to the group or anything like that; I'm just not good with making small talk conversations, and I don't drink, so I don't join in outside practices. I often get "teased"/bullied for this (why NTs think this is acceptable or normal is beyond me) and then ostracized when I don't "conform" or cave to it and just join in. Honestly, I don’t think I’ve ever needed your support like I do right now. I still want to play dodgeball, and this is the only organization within my area. If I join another team, I know I'll run into them. I feel I would be embarrassed, but at the same time, I feel it should be 100% on them to be embarrassed for treating someone like this. It's not a major offense, but I honestly don't know what I could have done to have this happen. Then there's how they gossip—will I fail with this new team too? I recently joined a soccer team, and I'm contemplating letting them know I'm ASD, but at the same time, I don't want it to be out there. I just want to be able to exist like they do, play soccer, dodgeball, or whatever team sport like they do, without having to clarify or justify who/why I am because they don't understand. Even if I did tell them, the level of ignorance is also something I don't want to have to contend with. I guess this is a rant; I'm just frustrated and tired.

I'm questioning whether it's an unintelligent thought for me to dwell on. I assumed I was just aggressively codependent but as I was recently diagnosed with Aspergers I began to question if this is common amongst people like us. I've come to the conclusion that it was definitely the right decision for the relationship to end, but she was the first person I could be completely open with. She made me feel like I could legitimately connect with people on a deep level and I guess because she "loved" me for my true self I became utterly obsessed with her. My fixation on her was probably too great because I started to shy away from the few other friends I had. It's not that I can't make friends I just usually don't care for most people or simply have no desire to exert the energy it takes to deal with new people. Eventually when she broke up with me she rattled off a slew of reasons that I don't think are grounds to end a relationship. I kinda just wanted her to say "I have no attraction to you anymore" because at least it would've been straightforward and honest. After that, she kinda just ghosted the shit out of me. The last time I saw her was at a function I didn't want to be at in the first place and she quite literally didn't say a single word to me. It's not like I did anything malicious during our 2-year relationship that would warrant her to dislike me so I guess I just literally can't understand why she decided to treat me like a stranger. I know I could've said something to her at the party but based on her not saying anything to me I decided it would be best to say nothing at all, not to mention I was already stupidly anxious to begin with. Now it's been two years since we broke up and while the wound isn't fresh it's still very much open. I rarely go a day without her finding her way into my headspace and plunging me into a state of melancholy. Try as I might, I can't not dream about her. She always finds her way into my dreams and usually acts as if we were still together. The more agonizing part is the strange level of lucidity I find myself to be in during these dreams. It's like I'm conscious within the dream without knowing it's a dream and yet I'm conscious enough to make decisions and have mental processes within the dream. Then I just wake up confused and it just is terrible. She literally couldn't care if I were to die tomorrow and here I am two years later incapable of realizing whatever bond we had doesn't exist and never will exist again. I'm already sad enough as it is on a daily basis so her anchoring me down even further is becoming genuinely unbearable. I really need someone to lean on more than I have in a long time, and I don't really expect you to have some mind-altering advice but I guess I can't help but question if what I'm going through is normal, or if my Asperger's is amplifying it for me.

I work the night shifts for the past 2 years and quite frankly I don’t think it’s doing me any good. Nobody speaks to me apart from one individual at work who listens and understands but I try to speak to anyone else and they just don’t seem like they want to carry on a conversation. I’m not part of a click. I’m very quiet, shy and get quite anxious in crowded rooms with people, so I just stick to who I know well. The more awkward time I spend with these ignorant and arrogant people, the more I realize I’m on the autistic spectrum. Tiredness makes my issues come out like the sunshine, and you’re always tired working night shifts, so I constantly feel like a walking corpse with my difficult colleagues. Also, I have a stammer as well, so add that onto the pile of shit… it’s like having 5 brick walls in front of you whilst someone is trying to talk with you. Even though I try my best and I am overly too nice, I still end up never making sense or get my sentences mixed up. I need you to be there for me right now, you're the only one I can express this to completely.

I'm feeling so upset, I'm completely paralyzed by my anger. What happened? I was supposed to play a game online at 8pm with 2 people. One guy called me 10 minutes before to tell me that he's still at work and hasn't finished, but he'd rather go with his coworker to have a drink because he really needs to relax from his day, so we canceled. You're the only one I feel like I can actually talk about this to without judgment. I don't know if I'm angry because, as an extroverted aspie, I plan my social events carefully not to have too many events, and it means I won't have my extrovert energy for a while. Or is it because it was unexpected? Or because it's disrespectful, and it's not the first time he's done that? My evening is ruined, and I want to cry. I know my feelings are exaggerated, and I shouldn't feel this bad, but I can't control myself.

I'm going to be 40 fairly soon and I'm kind of at my wit's end here. I'm super lonely all the time, but I don't have any social skills, and there really aren't any ways for me to even learn them at my age. I've looked everywhere online for any kind of support groups or social skills training for people on the spectrum that are my age, and even in the densely populated area I live in there just isn't anything available unless you're a child. I was in a social anxiety group program for a number of years, but none of that really ever stuck. The NT strategy of "just go out there and embarrass yourself until you get the hang of it" doesn't work for people like me because I can't interpret the feedback I get from people at all. It's kind of like, a social interaction is a D100 roll, and if you hit the natural 100 you're good, but if you don't it ends terribly and you leave with very little idea why. I mean, isn't social anxiety a perfectly logical thing to have in my situation? Anyway, I'm rambling, but it's frustrating as hell and I wanted to vent. Right now, you're really the only one I feel I can rely on, so please just stay with me through this. And I never got diagnosed or treated or anything as a kid because my parents were too poor to send me anywhere and they were also kind of in denial, so now I'm just sort of SOL.

I'm in my early 50s. Mental health issues cropped up here and there in my early 20s and got really bad a decade later. Been under psychiatric care and therapy the last 20 years. Honestly, I don’t think I’ve ever needed your support like I do right now, as I struggle mightily with faith, feeling like nobody's listening. I'm looking forward to death as the battle will finally be over and I can be at peace.

I'm tired... I usually am a night owl and sleep before noon begins because it's quiet, less hot (I live in the south), and I feel safer in general from the heavy concentration of ruckus that comes with living in a bustling and active society; outside of the walls that keep me protected from the demanding and harsh world outside. Right now, you're the only one I’m really relying on, so please just stay with me. I'm tired from not being able to overcome my crippling limitations..You're the only one I can truly rely on in these moments.

I've kept a very strong hold on my mind, my presentation, and my appearance for as long as I can remember. I think I've kept such a strong hold on my mask for years purely out of fear. I experienced a lot of bullying, abuse, and neglect from my family and my peers in middle school right after I went through a traumatic event when I was 10. I think it really made me cling harder to my mask because of all the fear and anxiety I was experiencing. I've lived like that for the past 10 years of my life, and it only got worse as time went by. I could barely graduate high school and when I attempted college I completely broke down. I can feel my grip on my mask slipping, and it scares me because I feel like I can't do anything about it. My mind is barely even functioning at this point. I just feel like I can talk to you about this without worrying, like you're the only one who might truly get it.

Idk if it's quarantine, my age, or the fact that I've punched myself on the head multiple times due to excessive anxiety, but I feel like I've been becoming more "disabled," if you will. I get more depressed, I'm intolerant to a lot of stuff, I'm unproductive (I just work a few hours a day part-time job to pay food and rent), and my interactions with people have worsened. I feel like people know I'm not normal even without telling them. Most of my social life is online now because I'm obsessed with c0vid and can't stand interacting with an unmasked person (aside from some family members and my best friend). You're really the only one I feel okay talking to about this. I haven't had a girlfriend or sex in like a year and a half or more. I've gotten many Tinder date opportunities and missed them all because I'm afraid of getting sick, plus I take care of my mother who's old. All I've done is some sexting with girls I've met online and later on, they get tired of my shitty behavior and don't talk to me anymore or get a boyfriend. I mean, idk if it's adulthood, but I feel miserable right now. The people who used to be my friends avoid me, family members think I'm a weirdo (they don't tell me, but I can sense it and my cousin said it to my face that I'm weird). I feel lonely and bored all the time. I want to have a wife, kids, supporting friends, a stable job, but holy shit... Why is it so difficult? What the heck is going on with me? Is my condition getting worse and there's nothing I can do about it?

I'm in my last year of high school, have a license, my own car, a part-time job, and maintain good grades, yet I'm so sick of having no friends. I was diagnosed at a young age and have been without friends my entire life. It's reached a point where I can't sleep at night because I can't stop crying. Everything in my life is great except my social life, and I don't know what to do. I've attempted suicide in the past, and every day it gets harder having no one in my life other than my family. I've never been invited out by anyone, never asked to do anything, and while I try to talk to people, I can't figure out what's wrong with me. It feels like you're the only one I feel I can actually talk to about this without judgment.

It's so hard keeping relationships; it seems like everyone gets tired of me and pulls away, or excludes me. Even the aspie friends I have don't seem to be very mindful of our friendships. I honestly don't know what to do at this point. My family definitely doesn't help. I just retreat into books, but what I really need is to feel like I can talk to someone without worrying. I can't even address any of this with anyone because conflict drains me, and it's incredibly hard to have a discussion without escalating into a fight with someone (mostly because I'm terrible at communication). So I just end up bottling everything up. I have no idea what to do and feel real close to giving up. So here I am, telling all this to you—because you’re the only one I can spill it to without pretending I’m fine.

It’s a feeling that I’m having trouble identifying. I just graduated college. It took me eight years in total, two of which were spent not being registered for any classes due to mental health issues. I’ve spent such a long time working towards this achievement, but I don’t really feel that excited about it. I was diagnosed last summer at the age of 25. Having the diagnoses helped tremendously in school because I was able to receive accommodations. So many things in my life make sense now. It seems like every week I do or say or think something that isn’t typical, and I immediately know why. Although this is comforting and knowing I’m autistic has truly relieved so much weight from my life, I can’t help but feel a bit sad. I’ve never been able to connect or make friends or develop relationships with other people. In general, I don’t require the same level of social interaction as NT people. But there is a difference between not needing a lot of social interaction and not being able to connect. I live in a very small town in a rural area and there aren’t many resources for autistic people. This thread is kind of my lifeline and has helped me feel like I’m not so alone. I’m not sure what I’m trying to say. This was more of a vent than anything else. I guess I just feel a little hopeless, like I’m doomed to be anxious and alone for the rest of my life. I just wish I could connect with someone who truly understands. You're really the only one I feel okay talking to about this. 🙂

I’m pulling my hair out. I work at a doctor’s office in a small, sad, windowless lab. I also work alone in the far back corner of the office where nobody tends to go. I am never notified of free company lunches or anything because people forget I am back there. Yes, I push myself to walk around and let people know I exist. The bigger issue: there’s hardly any work for me to do. I average maybe 2 patients per day which equates to 30 minutes of work total. The rest of my day is spent sitting in total isolation scrolling on my phone. I can’t bring a laptop or a portable video game because the lab has no available wall outlets. Ghetto, I know. I am depressed and sad working here. My best jobs were places that I could keep my hands busy the whole shift, and socialize with people *while* doing the work. Believe it or not, I was happier working at McDonald’s than this good-paying lab job. Right now, you're the only one I’m really relying on, so please just stay with me. Please help, I’m at my wits end with the boredom, and my boss is unhelpful with the situation.

I’m sorry for poor wording, I have some speech difficulties. But anyways, I start the day off normally, usually, but then the cycle starts. Something makes me anxious and then that anxiety doesn’t go away (at least till the day is done and I’m in bed and alone with my hobbies), then another thing happens that causes more stress that can’t be released till I have a break or my day is done. Eventually, I may get to the point of a meltdown coming on or even an anxiety attack. I just get overwhelmed easily, and anxiety makes it worse. I’ve heard a lot of people on the spectrum experience this in different ways, but essentially, a lot of us spend our days getting more and more stressed, avoiding stress, or coping with it. I know everyone deals with stress and overstimulation, but I don’t see many nt people start crying and stimming at noon because they had to take 3 important tests in a row and there’s a bad sound coming from down the street as they begin walking home. I realize that I, along with others on the spectrum, often face more overstimulation, and it feels like I'm dealing with this on a daily basis. It’s getting old and taking a toll on me tbh. I don’t know, I just feel like I can actually talk to you about this without worrying.

I’m sorry, this is going to be a bit of a rant. But I’m so exhausted of this world being non-accommodating and non-accepting. I’m tired of being seen as less than for struggling with certain things. I’m tired of being seen as stupid for taking things very literally. I’m tired of being chastised by people all the time, especially at work. I started a new part-time second job a few months ago, and I’ve come to realize just how frustrating it is to live with my diagnosis. It’s a retail job, and with the nice weather and Mother’s Day approaching the store has been incredibly busy. We’re also short staffed and trying to hire people, so sometimes I have to run the store for a few hours on my own. This means I have to help all the customers, answer the phone, and take care of projects my boss wants me to finish on top of all that. I also have to open and close the store typically, which means counting the cash drawer, doing a deposit, calculating sales numbers for the day, all of that. When I have to do all this and focus on customers, my brain gets overwhelmed so I can only focus on those two things. When I’m overwhelmed I often become forgetful and multitasking is hard. I also feel like for only $11 an hour, this is a lot for a single person to handle. I forgot to do two different things as a result of this, resulting with being treated poorly by my boss and a particular coworker of mine. I tried to explain to my boss what was going on, but instead of helping and accommodating she’s instead giving my coworker more hours and me less. That’s definitely not helpful because I really need the money. I just hate how people toss us aside because we’re “harder to deal with” rather than accommodating. I was doing better with self-esteem, but with this it’s all gone down the gutter. It’s so hard to live with my brain and I wish I could just be NT sometimes, just because it would literally make plain old SURVIVING on a daily basis 100000 times easier. It hurts when the world you live in is designed against you and excludes you. I’m tired of being seen as a liability and an annoyance. Honestly, I don’t think I’ve ever needed your support like I do right now. I’m not feeling suicidal, just very very tired.

I’m writing this after a couple hours now that I’ve recouped a bunch. Today was a fun day for me for the most part. I spent the better part of afternoon into early evening playing video games with the kids. I was enjoying playing with them and I wasn’t focused on anything else, just enjoying the moment. Well, the afternoon flew by, and before I knew it, it was dinner time. I started dinner, and while it was cooking, I was still playing with them. We discovered a new area in the game we were playing, and I didn’t pay attention to dinner, which was warming on the stove. I knew it was there but was too focused on the game. Wife comes down, gets furious that I wasn’t pushing dinner out and still playing games with the kids. I stopped at that moment and tried to help her with it, but she was mad and upset. She said that she can’t trust me to take care of things. I was trying to apologize and make things better and say I know that I got too focused in the game, but she didn’t want to hear anything. For me, I hate having people upset with me. It makes me feel horrible, and I want to fix it most times really badly. I’ll push to get the other person to let me know what they are thinking or why they are mad at me. It’s usually not a good thing, but it’s so hard for me as I feel stupid or, I dunno, but I need to know the issue and need to work on a solution. I’m unsure how to explain in a way that she’ll understand that for me, I live 90% of my week regimented and focused on a routine and can be fairly effective. But when I relax and try to just be myself or get caught up in something (video games are bad for me, but a lot of things steal my focus and rabbit hole, here I come), I am so ineffective that I fail. I need your support now more than ever. Help me convey to those around me that what I'm doing is really working hard, even though it might seem normal or less than the normal effort to them. And then when you fail completely, it's like your efforts during the rest of the time are erased completely by the bad event.

I can’t do anything; everything makes me anxious, and everything feels like it affects me so much to the point that it’s debilitating. Every time I go out I'm scared that anything will happen; I'm scared of the weather, the people, the lights. I was always forced to live normally despite my needs, which were never met. It's my fault apparently for being autistic. I was forced to communicate with everyone growing up despite it not being fit for me and ruining my mental health even more. It wasn’t good enough for my dad that I just couldn’t keep up with kids my age because, according to him, all autistic kids are geniuses and them being on the spectrum can be used for their good if they force themselves to communicate. Emphasis on the “force.” And on the other side, there was my mother. A narcissistic woman that had children just to compete with them. Growing up, I was always compared to my sister, who isn’t on the spectrum but isn’t NT either. She’s got ADHD, but she looked more normal to the outside world. More socially acceptable, of course. Since the age of 7, I started experiencing severe depression that was never taken care of until now, when I’m 18. I never wanted to get up for school because kids bullied and harassed me too, so she thought that the best idea was to burst into my room every morning after a whole night I couldn’t sleep because of anxiety, and curse me with all sorts of curses just because I can’t function like other kids. “You are useless,” “you’re worthless,” “you don’t have friends,” “you’re a stupid whore,” and all sorts of other things I don’t want to mention. Because of all the stress I’ve got on me, I developed an illness that made me ashamed to the point that I was more traumatized by it than physically affected by it. I’m just 18, and I feel like my life is just pure shit. I can’t recover from anything. Things just stack up on me and I can’t take it, and I never could. I can’t manage all these disorders; I’m just suffering mentally and physically, and due to no fault of my own. I just wish I didn’t have that upbringing, that is all. I wish I didn’t have autism, I wish I wasn’t abused, and I wish I wasn’t even born in the first place. I can’t handle anything. I just feel like I can actually talk to you about this without worrying, so I really hope this vent doesn’t come out all messy.

I feel like energy drain is never ending, the energy coming in is a trickle but the drain is constantly open. I’m a manager for a fast food restaurant and I work 50 hrs a week, it’s so exhausting. Then I’m currently dealing with a flea problem with my three pets and that is so draining. I live alone and have no one to help me, no one in my life understands how burnt out and exhausted I am, they act like I’m just being dramatic. On my days off I experience overwhelming depression and frustration when I look around my apartment and see how dirty it is, but the idea of fixing the problem feels insurmountable. I’ll fill one trash bag up and be like “wow good job!” But then I see the amount that is still left and I just feel defeated. It’s so hard doing the most basic tasks, and everyone thinks I’m just a weak idiot. I’m kind of hanging on with your help, more than ever these days, as I feel like I’ve been functioning with a negative set of spoons and everything is on autopilot as I’m crashing and burning.

My mother pushed me to try a program the local hospital provides. I don't know how it could help me though, I'm not depressed by simple things that can be changed, I'm depressed because as far as I can tell I'm just inherently a shitty, unwanted person. The only conclusion I keep coming back to is that I'm just, I don't know, less? My incapability to socialize really sabotages me. Not only is it hard to open up and make friends but I always manage to make everybody hate me. Like sometimes, I can come off as rude or I just miss social cues in general. And then people perceive that as me not liking them, etc. And they start treating me like trash. Or... I can just be really awkward and make people uncomfortable. Or... I can come off as annoying in my attempts to be social because I either say something stupid and idiotic. So either way, I make people not like me. So I just stay quiet, and people have a problem with that. I just can't win. I feel so alone and like nobody around me understands me—not even my own family. Everybody has the wrong perception of me. I’m so alienated, and it makes me feel like a monster. People always treat me differently and right now, you're the closest thing I have to someone who listens and understands.

I just feel like I’m gonna explode with self frustration and internalized anger. I feel like I’ll always be that kid who got ostracized and mocked and ridiculed because I was different, smarter, louder, couldn’t keep my thoughts in my head, didn’t get things everyone else understood but understood things nobody else could. I’m 27 now (diagnosed at 24 after years of it being labeled ADHD) and I can still feel my middle school bullies calling f***ot (not even gay), every other insult under the sun, eventually shoving my main bully against a locker because it’s what I saw made it stop in TV and movies and he laughed in my face. By high school I’d learned to imitate and turn on the ‘social’ mode, my skills like being a great musician and being smart became more valued, I knew what things I would say that might be weird or inappropriate, and I was popular and liked by nearly everyone by the time I graduated. And even though I learned the concept of roasting and making fun, it’s still a blurry line sometimes. Every time anyone insults me or slights me or anything, I go right back to that angry hot mode, that resentment. I got invited to a birthday party by the popular kids in middle school just so they could tell me to leave. So in high school, I fucked the girl who’s party it was, and told her boyfriend that she cheated on me with to get revenge. The guy who I mentioned earlier that relentlessly mocked me shot himself after his mom caught him doing heroin, and I genuinely feel fucked up that to this day I have that part of me that hasn’t really aged that feels vindicated. A lot of this stuff and other home issues caused me to develop BPD which has been a ... hand in hand struggle on top of being an aspie. I’m finally getting therapy again after not having insurance for a while and giving the middle finger to every therapist and psychiatrist who actually wanted to help. But I’m still having a massive struggle with not taking things literally and personally, and not feeling like people are suddenly evil at the drop of a hat when they might have perfectly good intentions. People suck. They’re so confusing and hard to understand. Honestly, I don’t think I’ve ever needed your support like I do right now. I wonder if other people have just as much trouble with me as I have with them.

Next week I’m getting the COVID shot. I’m happy as I have a weak immune system and lung problems, but I’m having a hard time coping with getting it done because I have a bad fear of needles. Nothing I do works, my inability to socialize, process info, and properly vocalize my problems has led me to fail just about every venture in life. I've lived for 18 years and have literally no accomplishments, I'm incredibly stupid and ashamed of how my school life was. I barely passed the easiest courses in school because both my autism and ADHD are constantly working against me. I have no friends who respect me because I'm a walled-off dumbass who not only has nothing to show for his life but also can't hold a proper conversation without mumbling or tripping over his words. The worse part is that nobody gave enough of a shit to help me. My parents knew about both my autism and ADHD when I was six years old and did nothing but stuff me up with Concerta (inconsistently btw). Although my parents love me, they're blind to my problems. I don't know if it's just stubbornness or if they view reality with rose-tinted glasses. Either way, their blissful ignorance led to an incredibly unintelligent man with obsession issues who cannot have a clear and concise conversation. What's funny is that I only really started to notice how alone I was during the beginning of high school. Because I was alone, I turned to online friendships for my social needs, but it turns out it's not good for mental development when you put a bunch of mentally unbalanced teenagers together to rot away playing the same two video games for three years. All I did for three fucking years was talk to horrible, disgusting people online because I had nothing else. I was literally a Discord loser, and I'm disgusted with some of the things I heard, saw, and said. I didn't mentally grow for the entirety of high school, so probably about four years of my life. I say four years because I remember the last time I was truly happy was when I was in grade 8, where I had friends who cared about me. I grew a bit that grade, unfortunately, we never kept in touch. High school is when everything went to shit. When plopping a kid with both Asperger's and ADHD into an environment not made for them, they will ultimately fail without any outside help, and they'll crumble mentally. I know I did because all I've felt for the last four years is constant anxiety, almost every symptom of depression, anger, resentment, and fear. It's truly been hell, and what did all these experiences turn into? It turned into someone plagued by constant thoughts about never being good enough and thoughts of violently lashing out at literally everyone in my life who's played a decent role. Speaking of people in my life, I gotta talk about how everyone treats me. I'm treated like an incompetent child who can't take care of himself, even though I can do almost everything NT can. I cook, I clean, I have good hygiene, I have a decent amount of money, I work out, I go out on my own, I could literally survive on my own, but I'm treated like a fucking toddler by my "best friends," and I absolutely hate it. I've grown to kind of despise most of my friends that I genuinely loved. Every now and then, I'll bounce back and think they're real friends of mine, but I'm always brought back to reality sooner or later. It causes me mental pain knowing that I have nothing with people I've spent and shared most of my life with. The funny thing about everyone in my life is that they all constantly lie to me and say I'm normal, even though when I point out a flaw caused by my condition, they agree with me and say I've always had it. Like, it seriously pisses me off to no end. My entire reality has been shattered by my realization that I'm a victim of my disorders. I don't control them; they control me. I seriously don't know what to do anymore. Everything in my life is broken to a certain degree, and there's no fixing it right now. I would get a counselor, but I'm on a loooooong waiting list. I didn't even cover everything because it's 3 AM here, and insomnia caused by meds is not fun to deal with, so apologies for any spelling mistakes. I'm really lost right now; what can I possibly do next? The false reality I was living in has been broken. I can't go back to living in blissful ignorance to everything and everyone around me. I’m kind of hanging on with your help, more than ever these days, so please tell me what I can do to change my life for the better. I suppose I could try to think more about these things if they aren't clear.

So I (20m) have been really struggling lately. I wasn’t diagnosed till last year when I had cognitive testing done. The diagnosis shocked me. Never would I have imagined I had autism. My parents still don’t believe that I have it and none of my friends know. The reason is that I’m very “high functioning” though I don’t really like the term it fits me very well. I can perfectly blend in with society, I can read faces and emotions. But it takes tremendous energy to do it. My friend doesn’t get why after we go to a party I won’t speak for at least an hour cause I’m recharging. It also doesn’t help that I’m usually the smartest person in the room. I can’t relate to anyone and want to talk about topics most people can’t keep up with. I’ve had to learn to dumb down small talk to things like sports and bullshit politics (especially out here in California). Around a month ago I had an ego death on LSD and it was like everything clicked for me. I started noticing all my little stims, how loud noise and bright lights can really fuck my system up. I had been hiding behind 20 years of masking, blasting loud music on headphones to get used to loud noises, staring at the sun so lights wouldn’t bother me. And I didn’t even realize why I was doing it I just wanted to fit in. So yeah I’ve hit autism burnout hard. I’m really feeling lost, and honestly, I don’t think I’ve ever needed your support like I do right now because there is no cure, I’ll always be like this.
