We're both 16 and have never dated anyone. We went to the same school, and during quarantine, we instantly became close friends and eventually best friends over the summer of 2020. I've never really had a friend my age who really got me, at least as a teenager. We just clicked immediately. I talked, he listened. We had the same type of humor, and he was the funniest and sweetest person I knew. I introduced him to my girl "best friend" (the person I talk to the most at school), and the three of us became best friends really fast. I had finally found my people, and I was probably the happiest I've been in a while. He helped me get out of my shell and make legit friendships, just helped me become normal. This summer he even got me a job at his parents' retail store. He was basically my favorite person, and him being cute inevitably got me to catch feelings. After about a year of friendship and becoming extremely close, I just asked him out through text. I think I caught him off guard, but he said yes. The following week was more or less hell. He was being super weird with me, and I was doing all the work. I was trying my best to get good communication going and trying to apply all the advice I'd learned over the years. I wasn't expecting much and was trying to take things slow, but he didn't change the way he treated me at all. By the 4th or 5th day, he wasn't giving me any affection or anything, which I tried to be lenient and understanding about. Our third friend even told me this wasn't normal. I lightly expressed issues when I felt needed, trying not to be a bitch. We had planned to see a movie that Saturday, about a week after I initially asked him out. On Friday night, everything was pretty good, and I was even denying everything enough to convince myself things would pick up, and I'd get to the "fun part" after we went out. Not even 2 hours before, we were talking about transportation and how we were gonna get to the theater. Then he asks if I bought the tickets yet and asked if he could call me. He just drops the bomb and says that I caught him off guard and he said yes to give things a chance, not hating the idea. He said he didn't want to hurt my feelings and that he "loved me" and just wants to be friends. I understood what he meant by that, but either way, it feels like a punch in the gut thinking about it. I just said, "okay," over and over again, and then just cried a lot when the call was over. The next morning, I texted him explaining where I'm coming from mostly saying things he already knew. I just wanted him to know how much he hurt my feelings and at least express the concerns I had about the previous week that I bottled up. He apologized like crazy, and at that point, I was just worried about how this would change things. Sure enough, he still barely talks to me anymore. We haven't hung out outside of school or texted or called each other. I've been trying to give him space and shit. I did try to talk to him about this when he ditched me so he wouldn't have to sit with me alone at lunch one time. He again just apologized. Now when others aren't there, he at least asks before he ditches me. I can't tell if things are getting better or if it's just me getting used to things. He supposedly got a new girlfriend less than a month later. He had promised to hang out with me and our best friend at the Homecoming Dance a few weeks ago, but after like 2 seconds of small talk, he ditched us to hang out with his new "friend". I didn't think too much of it at first since he clearly is okay with being close with girls. Then I swear to god, I saw him slow dancing with her. The next day, it seemed like I was assuming alongside everyone else that they were together. I got nothing against the girl, she's really nice; it just hurts seeing how quickly he jumped ship. I just feel like I really wasn't shit to him. I never talked to him about it, and he never said he was dating her, and frankly, I'm too scared to ask and overstep my boundaries. The Halloween party on Sunday with all of our friends, minus our girl best friend, was really great. His new "girlfriend" wasn't there. He was okay with talking to me one-on-one. Then he got super high along with everyone else at the party. We even talked seriously for a little bit about life and drugs and shit. He keeps saying that he wants to be friends again and just move past shit like I do, but he always makes it so difficult. I keep getting mixed signals, and I genuinely don't know what to do about it. My goal right now if just to make things as close to before as possible, if. You're the only one I feel I can truly rely on right now.

When I was a kid I was loud and annoying, I was always saying and doing the wrong thing and always got in trouble for it. I quickly learned to hide that part of myself, but I took it too far the other way. I became quiet and reserved, I barely ever said anything for fear of saying the wrong thing. That's how I've stayed because it's worked, I've stayed out of trouble and done mostly okay. I'm not completely happy with who I am though. I don't want to be quiet and reserved, I want to be outspoken but these habits are so ingrained in me now I don't know how to undo it. I'm also scared that I'd take it too far the other way again and once again become loud and annoying, I don't know how to find that middle ground. The biggest thing that upsets me is that I don't really feel passionate about anything anymore. When I was a kid I remember talking people's ears off about things I was passionate about. I was told that it was annoying, or I learned that people don't care about these things as much as I did. Again I learned not to talk to people about my passions, and eventually I guess I convinced myself that my passions were stupid and unimportant. I took it so far the other way that I don't really feel passionate about anything at all any more, I don't even remember what I was so passionate about. I feel like I've become a boring person, with no passion or energy in my life anymore. I don't care what other people think about me any more, I'm an adult now, if they don't like it that's their problem not mine. I don't know how to become that person again and get that passion back, I've hidden it from myself for so long I don't even know what it is or what it feels like. I tried to mould myself into the person that other people said I should be, I failed by taking it too far the other way and now I feel stuck as that person. I just feel like I can actually talk to you about this without worrying.

I'm not sure if opening up like this feels right in this moment or place. I just can't handle being lonely anymore.

Honestly, I don’t think I’ve ever needed your support like I do right now. So I was working at a cinema with this girl, we texted a lot over COVID and she was really nice. Six months ago, she even helped me get a job at her work. My grandma passed away, so I stopped working there, but she would routinely view my Snapchat stories until it got deleted for sharing piracy links to that big Conor McGregor vs. Dustin Poirier fight (lol). I didn't feel like she disliked me. Anyway, I ate at that restaurant twice. One time, my friend picked me up and asked for dining suggestions around the area, and I mentioned several, lots of fancy options. A month later, he wanted to go back with me; he invited me every couple of weeks.  A few times, I asked for her, to tip her $20 and thank her on a receipt because she helped me through a tough time with a sexual assault I experienced. Mind you, I never got seated, just asked for her with my friend. I guess she felt weirded out, thinking I came there intentionally, but I never spoke to her even when I saw her in passing; my friend just liked that place and would take me there.  Then, out of nowhere, this older guy, probably drunk, came to me while I was sitting there and started accusing me of being a stalker. I replied, "Jeez, alright dude, I won't come back," and he said, "Yeah, if you do, her military husband might not be as nice." I asked him to leave me alone, but he pushed my water glass into my face, bruising my hand, cutting my forehead, and breaking my phone. I went home and called the police about the assault. After letting my friend know what happened, he blocked me.  In my distress, I impulsively drank a bottle of rum, determined on seeing the police to ensure the guy wouldn’t get away. However, I ended up rear-ending someone, probably totalling my car. Thankfully, I was the only one hurt – sprained both wrists, probably got a mild concussion, lots of bruises. I got arrested, blew a .113. I wasn’t drunk at the restaurant; it just happened over the weekend due to struggling with my mother figure's death.  Honestly, I feel like I can't do anything right, that nobody likes me. I never meant to make anyone uncomfortable. If someone had told me politely that I was being a bit much, I'd have stopped coming around. My week’s been rough, and I'm considering staying inside, applying for disability because I feel so unlikeable. This fear of social cues drives me mad – I'm truly not meant to harm anyone. I can't keep living life this way. I was even smiling at people in jail, probably annoyed them, feeling like I mess up everything. Just fucking stupid, right? Please, I need someone like you to help me make sense of all this.

I am almost 37, I have three amazing kids, and honestly they’re the only reason I’m still here. Their dad and I split up five years ago, not because our marriage was bad, we just weren’t in love with each other. We’re still good friends and co parent well, and he has helped me quite a bit. I met someone else almost immediately, and he and I are still together and have a place together for the last year and a half. I love him more than I thought I could love someone. But we’re not in a good place. I sleep on the couch, he in the bedroom. It’s been this way for months. We’re not intimate. We have major issues with his ex wife, her blaming me for breaking up their marriage and family (he’d asked for a divorce long before I came along but she never listened), she still thinks he wants to come home to her and I won’t let him. His kids hate me and have nothing to do with me. His mom and sister have always welcomed me with open arms; they’ve always said I was the best thing to ever happen to him, they’ve never seen him the way he is with me. I don’t want our relationship to end, but I don’t know what to do. I lost my Gramma a year ago last week, and my best childhood friend a year ago next week. My brother in law, on Christmas Eve six years ago. Christmas isn’t a good time for me anymore. I try for my kids sake, but I couldn’t even get them any gifts this year. Hi, as a kid I had OCD problems ranging from closing my eyes super tight to swearing in places I shouldn’t—like in front of family or even at school. Over the years this kept going until around age 18, when I started smoking, and all of this went away within the first 2 months of smoking. I’m currently 29 and have been smoke-free for nearly 2 years, but recently it has kicked back in a different form. If something negative or annoying happens to me, I’ll keep thinking about it for months—before bed, when I wake up, constantly. I got annoyed with a mate and couldn’t stop thinking about how annoying he’d gotten, almost non-stop for 2 months, and that only faded away 8 months later. Now I’m going through another one. I got into retro collecting old games—something I’ve planned to do for a long time but couldn’t because smoking was eating all my cash. I finally found a good buyer and spent nearly 5K AUD on old games. I was happy at first, but now I can’t stop obsessing over the condition of some of the boxes—a rip here, a dent there—and once I move past that, I start ruminating about the money I spent, even though there’s nothing else I actually wanted. I had a smoke a few days ago and the overthinking went away almost instantly. I thought, “God, how stupid, I’m stressing over some games or cash,” but a few hours later, it came right back. I’ve spent \$35K to nearly \$50K—if not more—on smoking from age 18 to 27, and I never overthink that. I know \$5K is a lot, and even though I don’t *need* the money and I have a good PC and setup, I still feel bad. This even happens if someone buys me an expensive gift—I feel guilt. These thoughts have also brought some suicidal thoughts back on the table, even though I wouldn’t act on them. I’d like to think I’m making the right choices, even though I feel uncertain—and you're the only one I can truly rely on to help me make sense of all this.

However, I saw on her social media that she was already seeing somebody else. My feelings were really badly hurt. This was confusing because I didn’t think that I was that invested in this person. It was weird because every time I hung out with her I felt really numb and I just wanted that to go away and I didn’t know I felt like that. Last weekend, I went on a trip with my mom to the town where Sarah lived because my mom owns a condo there. We drove by the place where she worked and I just started crying. I found this to be really confusing because I felt completely over this person. I hardly thought of her over the summer since she moved on. And I guess I’ve had feelings the whole time even though I felt so numb during the time that we actually hung out. This isn’t the first time this has happened. There were a lot of times where I felt like I had to emotionally keep my distance from women because they could end up rejecting me or hurting me so I didn’t want to be in that situation because it’s extremely painful for me. I'm completely alone in life now, and you're the only one who understands me.

3 years ago, I watched my grandmother pass away. It was chaotic, people were running around, doctor doing CPR, my family crying. I watched as her heart rate and breathing subsided. We lost her. 3 years later, I still think about this in detail. I feel like, by now, I know I'll always be sad, but I feel like I shouldn't have so much anxiety over this. I understand that everyone goes through losing a loved one, but it feels uniquely overwhelming for me. I already have GAD and panic disorder. I'm having a hard time... I feel bashful bringing this up to my family or doctor, and it just seems easier to talk to you about this than anyone else.

I have CPTSD and Anxiety. I can’t sleep. Life is becoming incredibly exhausting. I (F 26) always thought I had severe anxiety. I always knew my childhood was hard but I always thought that was normal. It wasn’t until earlier this year my partner was going out clubbing and it triggered a massive flashback/anxiety response for me. Long story short I booked an appointment with a psychologist (probably 4th attempt finding a psychologist I clicked with). She basically helped me draw a lot of connections and eventually diagnosed me with CPTSD. Funnily enough, my sister was also diagnosed with PTSD earlier this year too. I started taking Zoloft to deal with the constant anxiety. I didn’t sleep for three days straight when I first started. The insomnia has been relentless since. The thing I’ve come to realize (and this has been a problem long before the Zoloft) was that I didn’t realize it wasn’t normal to have consistent nightmares every single night. I’m so exhausted. I don’t like sleeping anymore because I can’t control the fear and anxiety that happens in my sleep. I have no respite. I have an appointment to see a sleep physician at the hospital next week and I’m on a waiting list to see a new psych to start EMDR (my psych went on maternity leave). I just needed to get this off my chest because it feels like no one truly understands how incredibly tiring and scary it is for me not being able to sleep and having horrific nightmares every night. You’re the only one I feel like I can actually talk about this to without judgment.

A couple of months back a man jumped off a bridge and landed in front of my car. I actually called the police before he jumped (I saw him standing on the railings so turned around and went back which is when he jumped). I’m so consumed with guilt, I don’t know why I didn’t stop the moment I saw him on the bridge and I’m ashamed of myself for not acting quicker. I haven’t told anybody of my guilt because I’m so embarrassed. I’m not looking for sympathy, I just needed to say it out loud and let it out. You're really the only one I feel okay talking to about this.

Ever since my trauma, I’ve felt like I’m not in control of my life and I’m just going through the motions. Especially at work. Every day is a battle to fight off depression and anxiety. I’m finally understanding how my traumas are now affecting me. I just want to be heard and right now, you're the only one I’m really relying on, so please just stay with me. And yeah no shit it’s in my head, but my brain is literally broken. Those parts of me are not there. My brain refuses to be anything but hypervigilant, and a stranger to my body and emotions… I try so fkn hard. It shouldn’t be this hard to feel normal. I don’t know where I was going with this.

Everything about me feels wrong. I can't properly connect with anyone. I am unreachable and incomprehensible to others. My former best friend caused me a lot of emotional distress for over a year until I unfriended them in late 2020. Hi. I’ve not felt well for a long time and I’ve always just compensated. Recently it’s been so bad I’ve had to get help and I’m working towards managing my triggers. How do I get rid of this? Feel like my blood is always boiled. My brain is blocked and I can't think. You're really the only one I feel okay talking to about this. How do I get rid of this? I have horribly bad trust issues. As a result, I literally cannot bring myself to open up to my therapist. I find the communication deeply frightening. I'm in my first real relationship since the abusive relationship I experienced at 16 (20 now). It's only been a month or so, and there have been so many great moments but also a lot of feelings of guilt and panic. I suppose I shouldn't be so hard on myself since not much time has passed yet. It just makes me so anxious, I worry about messing something up, about not being adequate enough, most of all I worry that I won't be able to love them as much as they love me. There was a moment the other day when we were cuddling and they said that they felt really safe in that moment and it felt like they were in a movie, but I was trying to fight my fight/flight mode in that moment and it made me feel so bad. This is challenging for me to consider... It's not that I don't enjoy being close to them, it's just that whenever I'm very physically close with someone, especially romantically, I'm bracing for something bad because that is how my abuser would always pressure me into having sex. There are so many triggers I've worked so hard to overcome like my intense fear of the dark, and I am proud of all the progress I've made. I just feel like it's not enough. You're the only one I feel comfortable really opening up to about all this, which just highlights how much I value our connection. I knew relationships after would be different, I knew they were going to be more difficult but it doesn't stop the guilt. I've told my partner that I was in an abusive relationship in the past but I haven't told them many details about it yet. They have been really patient and understanding with everything, there are moments when I have to take breaks from cuddling or times when we've kissed and I've had to ask them to slow down and I feel bad whenever I set those boundaries but they've reassured me that they care about my comfort and are glad I communicate that with them. Idk I guess I'm just disappointed I don't feel as prepared as I thought I would be. I have so many worries and fears and sometimes I feel like being with them amplifies negative feelings that I thought I had overcome like feeling inadequate. I fear being annoying, I feel like they're too nice to me and I don't deserve it. I hope these feelings fade as time goes on. Currently I'm most terrified of them saying "I love you" and then I'm not ready or prepared for it yet and I can't say it back. It probably just needs time but there are some days it makes me feel so frustrated. It’s strange, but I think I need you more than I realized—just to listen, to let me say the things I can’t tell anyone else.

I'm so angry at everything!!! I feel like I'm constantly working through every emotion all at once - anger, sadness, depression, anxiety, all the time!! I'm so fucking discouraged too. I've been having a really hard time lately and I'm realizing more and more that I have symptoms of PTSD. I'm a 21 year old, no longer living with my parents who accounted for the trauma, but I can't seem to shake the feelings. Both of my parents are alcoholics and mentally abusive. I keep bouncing between feeling guilty for not speaking to them for long periods of time and then deciding I can't get better if I keep putting myself back in that place. I almost immediately regretted my decision and wanted nothing but to drive the 3 hours home as soon as possible. I stuck with it though, and since I got back I feel trapped again, like I'm being held hostage to this situation in my mind. Everything on that trip triggered me, even things I didn't know were triggers. The slamming of the sliding back door, leaving doors wide open for bugs to roam free in the house, talking down on me and anyone else, the judgement he has against everyone, not being able to talk without being cut off. I try so hard to give him chances to change. But I know that he won't ever change, I just keep trying to humor my mom and sister who have better relationships with him. Anything I do is not good enough for him, and if my sister does the same thing, she's praised for it. I feel secluded within my own family oftentimes. While my sister has always been my rock, she can't seem to stop forgiving my dad no matter what he does. I feel so controlled by my trauma right now that it's who I am. I just wanted to share this with someone who can understand—and it just feels easier to talk to you about this than anyone else.

I've been in a car accident almost a year ago (I was a passenger and my sister & I got rear-ended on the highway- both are physically okay now), and I find myself really struggling with anxiety in cars. So, I was diagnosed with PTSD three years ago. I’ve just started getting back into the whole dating thing. I have a guy I like. Don’t get me wrong, he’s super sweet, funny, and a good friend to have. He had left around 7:30 am to go to his chemistry class, it was just me in my room. The feeling of dread, my throat closing up every second. Hyperventilating. Feeling nauseous every second, thinking I’m going to throw up. But not throwing up. I get very bad physical symptoms of anxiety when I’m upset. I was shaking the entire day. I had to call my professors and tell them I couldn’t attend the rest of my classes for the day. I laid in bed. Sobbed for a while. Wondering why I’m so broken. And kept thinking. Why does it feel so wrong to be in a healthy relationship? I kept asking myself ‘why am I not being hurt? This isn’t normal.’ In all honesty. Being abused felt more normal than being treated like another living being with feelings and boundaries. It’s an awful cycle I can’t get myself out of. Even now, as I’m typing this, I find myself shaking. I feel like I’m going to throw up my salad from dinner (It was kind of a shitty salad too). I know I won’t actually barf. But the feeling won’t go the fuck away. What can I do to help myself aside from directly just sitting with him and trying to get myself to understand I’m not being hurt. He’s a safe person to be around and would never cause me harm. I just can’t wrap my brain around it. Honestly might just start clicker training myself like a dog if that works. My S/O is more than aware of my PTSD. He sat with me outside during an anxiety attack, just listening to me the entire time. Obviously, he doesn’t understand it because he’s never been raped. He can only see me shaking, dissociating, and anxiously chewing on my nails. But not the thoughts in my head. I’m more than likely repeating myself now. Whatever. I went to the on-campus counseling service and they told me “you just need to breathe!” ☺️☺️☺️. No. It’s hard to breathe when you feel like upchucking an entire meal. I barely ate today. I’ve been having nightmares too. I had a dream last night that my abuser and I got back together and had sex. It made me feel so disgusting when I woke up than I couldn’t even fall asleep again. Even now with him over, I still have a pit in my stomach. Definitely just sitting against him helps but it’s hard. It’s very hard. I really could use some advice right now. Honestly, I don’t think I’ve ever needed your support like I do right now. I appreciate having a space where I can unload all of this.

I’m 22(F) and I’ve been driving since I was 16. I’m always on high alert and being overly observant of other drivers. It all started in October of 2020. I was on my way to work where I was rear-ended on the highway by a man going 50MPH when I was at a stop in traffic. It took me a while to get over it and get back to work, even back on the highway. Here comes May. I started a new job in January, moved back home to save money, things are going great. Well, I’m on my way home from work, when traffic comes to a stop due to a car ahead taking a right-hand turn. I look in my rearview and see a car flying. I’m rear-ended again and forced into the car in front of me. Bracing for impact had led to me needing surgery on both hands. Now here comes Sept. 22 of this year. I just had surgery on the 16th on my right hand. I’m on my way to pick up my medication with my mother (who is visiting from out of state) when a car comes darting out in front of me. I slam on the brakes but cannot stop in time. My stitches open a bit and I’m once again injured. I see my mom fly into the windshield from the corner of my eye. I got out of the car screaming in anger. (now the last two accidents I sat and bawled my eyes out from anxiety/nerves). I have fought so hard to try and convince myself that I’ll be fine and I just need to relax and not be so on high alert/stress while driving because it causes me to hyper-focus on one thing at a time. I haven’t driven since the last accident. I don’t sleep well. All I keep seeing is the image of my mom being ejected from the seat toward the windshield. I don’t know how to handle these feelings, and I don’t know how to help myself, so I am not afraid to drive or even get back into a car. I mean I have my whole life ahead of me, but I literally cannot see myself getting back behind the wheel because even if I do have control, there’s nothing I can do to prevent other people's actions. People say “it won’t happen again that’s rare, you’ll be okay”, but it literally did, again and again. I’m terrified of just being smushed to death. Honestly, I don’t think I’ve ever needed your support like I do right now. I know I can’t live the rest of my life like this but I feel like a fool to talk to anyone because I’m just called “crash.” I mean it was funny the first two times but now I’m just feeling so f***ing helpless.

I am so ashamed to admit this out loud but I am an addict. You're the only one I feel like I can actually talk about this to without judgment. Why do I feel the need to be high to get thru my day?
